summary: Completed comprehensive testing of Party Sub-Ledger feature (Option B Upgrade). All 16 backend API tests passed (100%). Frontend UI tests passed - General Ledger dropdown shows all party account sections (Vendors, Customers, Employees), party ledger loads correctly when selected.
passed_tests: GET /api/finance/party-subledger/accounts returns 401 for unauthenticated users
GET /api/finance/party-subledger/outstanding-summary returns 401 for unauthenticated users
GET /api/finance/party-subledger/{account_id} returns 401 for unauthenticated users
GET /api/finance/party-subledger/accounts returns 200 with all party accounts
Party accounts grouped by type (vendors, customers, employees)
Filter by party_type=vendor returns only vendor accounts
Filter by party_type=customer returns only customer accounts
Filter by party_type=employee returns only employee accounts
include_control=true includes control accounts
Each party account has required fields (account_id, account_name, party_type, party_id)
GET /api/finance/party-subledger/{account_id} returns account details
GET /api/finance/party-subledger/{invalid_id} returns 404
GET /api/finance/party-subledger/outstanding-summary returns correct structure
GET /api/finance/general-ledger/accounts includes vendors, customers, employees
GET /api/finance/general-ledger with party account_id works
POST /api/finance/party-subledger/migrate-existing endpoint exists
GET /api/finance/trial-balance includes party accounts
Frontend General Ledger page loads correctly
Account dropdown shows Bank & Cash Accounts section
Account dropdown shows Accounts Payable (Vendors) section
Account dropdown shows Accounts Receivable (Customers) section
Account dropdown shows Employee Payables section
Selecting vendor account loads party ledger correctly
critical_code_review_comments: FIXED: Route ordering issue - /outstanding-summary was being caught by /{account_id} route. Moved outstanding-summary route before parameterized route.
Backend API correctly implements all party sub-ledger endpoints with proper permission checks
Party accounts properly grouped by type (vendor, customer, employee) in API responses
Control accounts (AR, AP, Employee Control) properly excluded from regular listings unless include_control=true
General Ledger dropdown correctly integrates party accounts with proper section headers
Frontend displays party accounts with balance information in dropdown
seed_data_creation: Migration already run - 19 vendor accounts, 28 customer accounts, 90 employee accounts created
context_for_next_testing_agent: Party Sub-Ledger feature fully tested. Backend endpoints: GET /api/finance/party-subledger/accounts (list all), GET /api/finance/party-subledger/outstanding-summary (summary), GET /api/finance/party-subledger/{account_id} (detail), POST /api/finance/party-subledger/migrate-existing (migration). General Ledger dropdown at /finance/general-ledger now shows vendors, customers, employees sections. All party balances are currently 0 as no transactions have been posted to party ledgers yet. Permissions: finance.party_subledger.view, finance.party_subledger.manage.

backend_api.GET /api/finance/party-subledger/account: WORKING - Returns all party accounts with grouping
backend_api.GET /api/finance/party-subledger/outstan: WORKING - Returns summary by party type

frontend_ui.party_ledger_display: WORKING - Selecting party account shows ledger with summary cards

bug_fix_applied.issue: Route ordering bug - /api/finance/party-subledger/outstanding-summary was being matched by /{account_id} route
bug_fix_applied.root_cause: FastAPI routes are matched in order of definition. The parameterized route /{account_id} was defined before /outstanding-summary
bug_fix_applied.fix: Moved /outstanding-summary route definition before /{account_id} route in server.py
